# MigrationGuard rules

<!-- Generated by `migration-guard rules --format markdown`. Do not edit by hand. -->

17 rules. Severity feeds the `--fail-on` gate; **Dialects** shows where a rule applies; **Fix** marks rules that `--fix` can rewrite automatically.

| ID | Severity | Name | Dialects | Fix | Summary |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| MG001 | HIGH | `add-not-null-column-without-default` | mysql, postgres | — | Adding a NOT NULL column without a default rewrites the whole table under a lock. |
| MG002 | HIGH | `create-index-non-concurrent` | postgres | auto | CREATE INDEX without CONCURRENTLY blocks writes to the table for the entire build. |
| MG003 | MEDIUM | `drop-column` | mysql, postgres | — | Dropping a column is a breaking change for deployed code still reading it. |
| MG004 | MEDIUM | `rename-column-or-table` | mysql, postgres | — | Renaming a column or table breaks running code that uses the old name mid-deploy. |
| MG005 | HIGH | `alter-column-type` | mysql, postgres | — | Changing a column type generally rewrites the table under an exclusive lock. |
| MG006 | HIGH | `set-not-null-on-existing` | postgres | — | SET NOT NULL scans the whole table under an exclusive lock to validate rows. |
| MG007 | HIGH | `add-validated-foreign-key` | postgres | — | Adding a validated foreign key scans every existing row while locking both tables. |
| MG008 | CRITICAL | `update-or-delete-without-where` | mysql, postgres | — | UPDATE or DELETE without a WHERE clause affects every row in the table. |
| MG009 | HIGH | `truncate` | mysql, postgres | — | TRUNCATE irreversibly removes all rows and takes an exclusive lock. |
| MG010 | MEDIUM | `drop-index-non-concurrent` | postgres | auto | DROP INDEX without CONCURRENTLY takes an exclusive lock on the table. |
| MG011 | HIGH | `add-primary-key` | postgres | — | ADD PRIMARY KEY builds a unique index and sets NOT NULL under an exclusive lock. |
| MG012 | HIGH | `vacuum-full` | postgres | auto | VACUUM FULL rewrites the entire table while holding an exclusive lock throughout. |
| MG013 | HIGH | `drop-table` | mysql, postgres | — | Dropping a table permanently deletes its data and breaks code still using it. |
| MG014 | HIGH | `cluster` | postgres | — | CLUSTER rewrites the whole table under an ACCESS EXCLUSIVE lock. |
| MG015 | HIGH | `mysql-modify-column` | mysql | — | MODIFY/CHANGE COLUMN can rewrite and lock the table depending on change and engine. |
| MG016 | HIGH | `reindex` | postgres | auto | REINDEX without CONCURRENTLY rebuilds indexes under an exclusive lock. |
| MG017 | MEDIUM | `lock-table` | mysql, postgres | — | Explicitly locking a table blocks other sessions for the whole transaction. |
